---
title: "Magenta Book Practice Implications"
description: "Practice synthesis for Magenta Book."
book_id: "magenta-book"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Practice"
citation_key: "hm_treasury_evaluation_task_force_2020_magenta_book"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:magenta-book:magenta-book-executive-summary:c0003"
  - "chunk:magenta-book:magenta-book-3-evaluation-methods:c0002"
  - "chunk:magenta-book:magenta-book-5-managing-an-evaluation:c0017"
source_status: "needs_review"
---
## Source

This practice synthesis draws on selected notes from *Magenta Book: Central Government guidance on evaluation* [@hm_treasury_evaluation_task_force_2020_magenta_book].

## Evaluation Practice Implications

- Scope evaluation questions before choosing methods.
- Use theory-based, experimental, quasi-experimental, qualitative, and economic methods proportionately.
- Build quality assurance and flexibility into evaluation management.
- Plan dissemination around users and decision timing.

## Applying the Notes

Use these implications as prompts during scoping, design review, method selection, and reporting. They should be adapted to the evaluation's decision context, available evidence, and user needs.

## Links

- [Executive Summary and Evaluation Types](section-01-executive-summary.qmd)
- [Choosing Evaluation Methods](section-02-evaluation-methods.qmd)
- [Managing an Evaluation](section-03-managing-evaluation.qmd)

## References
